channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk seeks government assurance for protesters ahead of legal proceedings
item.description: Climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike since July 22, 2026, expressed willingness to end his fast if the government assures no action will be taken against protesters. The pr...

item.title: Radio Free Alice Explore Isolation and Cultural Confusion in New Single 'Kick In The Shins'
item.description: Radio Free Alice have released their latest single, 'Kick In The Shins', which delves into themes of isolation and confusion within contemporary culture. The track, available on Atlantic Records, is a...

item.title: Justice Carpio Asserts Philippines' Sovereign Rights Over Ayungin Shoal Amid Tensions with China
item.description: Former Supreme Court Associate Justice Antonio Carpio has asserted that China cannot legally prevent the Philippines from constructing facilities on Ayungin Shoal, emphasizing the country's exclusive ...

item.title: Military disrupts terror logistics network by seizing medical supplies and arresting key suspects in Borno
item.description: The Nigerian military, through Operation HADIN KAI, successfully disrupted a terrorist logistics network by arresting five suspects and intercepting critical medical supplies in Borno State. The opera...

item.title: Former County Workers Face Delays in Retirement Benefits
item.description: Retired Nairobi County employees are raising concerns about persistent delays in accessing their retirement benefits. Through Eastleigh South Ward MCA Nicholas Juma, former staff are seeking answers o...

item.title: Dusseldorf Airport's vegetarian food options set a new standard for airport dining
item.description: Justine Costigan's review of Dusseldorf Airport highlights its innovative approach to catering to vegetarian and vegan travelers. While the airport's modern facilities and efficient layout are commend...

item.title: Corruption case: Tribunal orders repayment of R4m misused in water project scandal
item.description: A Gauteng-based company was found to have unlawfully received a R4 million grant from the National Lotteries Commission (NLC) for a community water provision project completed three years prior. The S...

item.title: US cotton exports and their impact on Africa's textile industry
item.description: The article discusses the implications of US cotton exports under President Trump's trade policies, highlighting both challenges and opportunities for sub-Saharan Africa's textile sector. It explores ...

item.title: Monitoring Claude Code context and usage limits with a new tool
item.description: The article explains how Claude Code, an AI model, hides context usage percentages and API limits from developers until they hit constraints. A new tool called cc-context-telemetry provides visibility...

item.title: A Toxic Love Story: The True Crime Documentary on the Anaheim Craigslist Case
item.description: Netflix’s new documentary, A Toxic Love Story, delves into the Anaheim Craigslist case, a harrowing true-crime saga that unfolded a decade ago. The film follows deputy US marshal Ian Diaz and his part...

item.title: Canada and U.S. Reach Agreement on Gordie Howe Bridge Revenue Sharing
item.description: Canada and the United States have finalized a revenue-sharing agreement for the Gordie Howe International Bridge, which will connect Detroit, Michigan, and Windsor, Ontario. The agreement outlines key...

item.title: Tanzania High Court Convicts Man in Husband's Murder Case
item.description: A Tanzanian man, Mwita Nyagabure, was convicted of murdering his neighbor John Habi after his wife, Pili Paul, testified that she witnessed the stabbing. The incident occurred on July 20, 2024, in Nya...

item.title: President Marcos commits to BARMM elections and peace initiatives
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has reaffirmed the government's commitment to advancing peace and development in the Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in Muslim Mindanao (BARMM), emphasizing the importance ...

item.title: Reforming Migration Policies: South Africa's Approach to Border Security and Visa Regimes
item.description: The article argues that South Africa's focus on securitizing borders to curb irregular migration is ineffective and costly. It highlights that most undocumented migrants are actually documented indivi...

item.title: Manila Ticket Prices for Byeon Woo-seok's 'The Secret Library' Announced
item.description: ABS-CBN News reported the official release of ticket prices for Byeon Woo-seok's literary event 'The Secret Library,' scheduled to take place in Manila. The announcement, made on July 22, 2026, highli...

item.title: Petrol Pump Owners Postpone Strike After Government Negotiations
item.description: The All Pakistan Petroleum Pump Owners Association (APPPOA) has postponed its planned nationwide strike for two weeks following successful negotiations with Petroleum Minister Ali Pervaiz Malik. Initi...

item.title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio Avoids Question on Trump's Chinese Election Interference Claims
item.description: During a press conference in the Philippines, U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio faced questions about whether he had discussed President Donald Trump's allegations of Chinese election interference w...

item.title: Ondo State Police Investigate Abduction of Two Farmers
item.description: Suspected gunmen have kidnapped two farmers in Ondo State's Akoko South-East Local Government Area, demanding a ransom of N10 million for their release. A family member revealed the incident during a ...

item.title: Canadians' Attitudes Toward AI Use in Education and Work Revealed in New Poll
item.description: A recent poll highlights Canadians' mixed attitudes toward artificial intelligence (AI) use in professional and academic settings. The survey reveals that most Canadians view AI integration in work an...

item.title: Kenyan TSC Teacher's Clean Payslip Sparks Debate on Financial Discipline
item.description: A photo of a Kenyan teacher's payslip showing zero loan deductions has gone viral, sparking debates about financial habits in the education sector. The image, shared on social media, highlights how un...
